c74object.name: fluid.chroma~
c74object.digest: A histogram of pitch classes in Realtime
c74object.description: This class computes a histogram of the energy contained for each pitch class across the analysis frequency range.

discussion.p: Also known as a chromagram, this typically allows you to get a contour of how much each semitone is represented in the spectrum over time. The number of chroma bins (and, thus, pitch classes) and the central reference frequency can be adjusted.

The process will return a multichannel control stream of size maxNumChroma, which will be repeated if no change happens within the algorithm, i.e. when the hopSize is larger than the signal vector size.
Read more about fluid.chroma~ on the learn platform.
fluid.chroma~ is part of the Fluid Decomposition Toolkit of the FluCoMa project. For more explanations, learning material, and discussions on its musicianly uses, visit flucoma.org.

objarg.name: numchroma
objarg.digest: Number of Chroma Bins per Octave

method.name: reset
method.digest: Return the object to its original state
method.description: Calling reset will change parameter values back to those the object was created with, i.e. any attribute values set in the box will be retained

description.p: FFT settings consist of three numbers representing the window size, hop size and FFT size in samples:
The hop size and fft size can both be set to -1 (and are by default), with slightly different meanings:

ul.li: For the FFT size, -1 = windowsize snapped to the nearest equal / greater power of 2 (e.g. windowsize 1024 => fftsize 1024, but windowssize 1000 also => fftsize 1024)
FFTSize, if != -1, will snap to the next greatest power of two > 4
The maximum FFT size is limited to the value of the maxfftsize argument
if FFT size != -1, then window size is clipped at FFT size

description.p: The highest frequency included in the analysis, in Hz.
Set an explicit upper bound on the FFT size at object instantiation. The default of -1 sets this to whatever the initial FFT size is

attribute.name: maxnumchroma
attribute.digest: Maximum Number of Chroma Bins per Octave

description.p: Manually sets a maximum value for numchroma. Can only be set at object instantiation. The default of -1 sets this equal to the initial value of numchroma
The lower frequency included in the analysis, in Hz.
This flag enables the scaling of the output. It is off (0) by default. (1) will normalise each frame to sum to 1. (2) normalises each frame relative to the loudest chroma bin being 1.

attribute.name: numchroma
attribute.digest: Number of Chroma Bins per Octave

description.p: The number of chroma bins per octave. It will determine how many channels are output per input channel.
The frequency of reference in Hz for the tuning of the middle A (default: 440 Hz)
Enable warnings to be issued whenever a parameter value is constrained (e.g. clipped)
